summary: W1: created migration 20260626000000 (backfill+NOT NULL ALTER, 7/7 green). W2: removed 4 redundant column-null guards (CS1-CS4); row-absence/DB-error guards preserved.

findings.claim: W1: Knex migration — make users.timezone NOT NULL with backfill
findings.evidence: New file created. up(): UPDATE WHERE NULL backfill then knex.raw ALTER TABLE MODIFY NOT NULL DEFAULT 'America/New_York' COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci. down(): knex.raw ALTER TABLE MODIFY NULL preserving DEFAULT+collation. 7/7 telly test assertions PASS.

findings.claim: W2-CS1: resolveTimezone() had redundant column-null guard `if (row && row.timezone)` — column is now NOT NULL
findings.evidence: Changed `if (row && row.timezone) return row.timezone;` to `if (row) return row.timezone;` at line 48. Surrounding try/catch (DB-error) and final `return DEFAULT_TIMEZONE` (row-absence) preserved unchanged.

findings.claim: W2-CS2: getUserTimezone() had redundant column-null fallback `(user && user.timezone) || 'America/New_York'`
findings.evidence: Changed to `user ? user.timezone : 'America/New_York';` at line 14. Row-absence guard preserved.

findings.claim: W2-CS3: getUserTimezone() had redundant column-null fallback `(user && user.timezone) || 'America/New_York'`
findings.evidence: Changed to `user ? user.timezone : 'America/New_York';` at line 91. Row-absence guard preserved.

findings.claim: W2-CS4: getUserTimezone() had redundant column-null fallback `(user && user.timezone) || 'America/New_York'`
findings.evidence: Changed to `user ? user.timezone : 'America/New_York';` at line 15. Row-absence guard preserved.
